falsification_rule_text: The hypothesis is considered falsified if the pre-registered empirical test shows the opposite direction of the claim at conventional significance (p > 0.10), or if the primary outcome measure moves less than 10% in the claimed direction across the sample. Exact thresholds will be pinned in the variables and estimator blocks when this stub is promoted from draft.
falsification_test_text: Callaway-Sant'Anna DiD on FRA firms 1978-1986: nationalised-1981/82 cohort vs matched private peers; TFP and ULC ATT |effect|<5% with p>0.10 supports null-efficiency-collapse claim.

variables_missing.name: nationalised_firm_indicator
variables_missing.source: constructed: binary = 1 for the 1981-82 nationalised firms (Saint-Gobain, Pechiney, Rhône-Poulenc, Thomson, CGE, BNP, CL, SG, Suez, Paribas, Indosuez)

variables_missing.name: nationalisation_event
variables_missing.source: constructed: event date = 1982-02-13 (Loi de nationalisation); secondary 1981-09 (announcement)
